SpecRule: A second SMB2 NEGOTIATE on an established connection must disconnect the connection.
Notes: Implemented with disconnect semantics; needs interop proof.

SpecRule: DialectCount must be non-zero.
Reject oversized dialect arrays to preserve bounds and spec sanity.
NegotiateContextOffset must be 8-byte aligned.
Dialect array must not overlap negotiate contexts.
SMB 3.1.1 negotiate must include exactly one PREAUTH_INTEGRITY context.
Notes: Duplicate handling exists; needs full wire coverage.

SpecRule: HashAlgorithmCount must be >= 1.
If no supported preauth hash overlaps, return STATUS_NO_PREAUTH_INTEGRITY_HASH_OVERLAP.
Current implementation only supports SHA-512.
Notes: Correct for current support surface, but must be documented.

SpecRule: Duplicate ENCRYPTION_CAPABILITIES contexts must be rejected.
Server must choose by server preference among overlapping ciphers.
Duplicate COMPRESSION_CAPABILITIES contexts must be rejected.
CompressionAlgorithmCount must be > 0.
Server should negotiate the supported overlap, not collapse to one ad hoc algorithm without full semantics.
Notes: Current code selects one algorithm and mishandles no-overlap/NONE semantics.

SpecRule: SigningAlgorithmCount must be > 0.
Server must choose the first client-offered signing algorithm it supports.
Notes: Needs interop proof with AES-CMAC, AES-GMAC, HMAC-SHA256.

SpecRule: Transport-level security capability must reflect actual secure transport state.
Notes: Current code latches client flag into conn->transport_secured.

SpecRule: TransformCount must be > 0 if context is present.
Advertised RDMA transforms must be tied to actual supported and enforced transforms.
Notes: Current code records client list without full supported-set intersection.

SpecRule: POSIX negotiate context indicates SMB3 POSIX support.
NetName may be validated against server name for compatibility diagnostics.
Notes: Current behavior logs mismatch but does not reject.

SpecRule: SMB 3.1.1 response must include PREAUTH_INTEGRITY context.
Include ENCRYPTION_CAPABILITIES when encryption is negotiated/supported.
Compression response must truthfully express negotiated compression behavior.
Response may include signing capabilities when signing was negotiated.
Response must only emit supported RDMA transforms.
Response must emit transport capabilities only on actually secured transports.
Notes: Current gating uses conn->transport_secured polluted by request context.

SpecRule: ServerGUID must remain stable across reconnect-sensitive flows.
ServerStartTime should reflect actual server start time.
Notes: Lazy initialization still needs race and persistence validation.

SpecRule: Signing-enabled and signing-required response bits must reflect server policy.
